summary: Executed backend pytest regression and frontend staged UX E2E on public URL. Form -> processing -> results flow, refresh/new actions, and /comparison + /chat navigation are functional.

ui_bugs.issue: Browser console repeatedly logs width(-1)/height(-1) warnings during chart render; no functional break observed but indicates render timing/layout instability.

action_items: Stabilize chart container sizing on first render to eliminate Recharts width/height console warnings.
Keep explicit UI messaging that AI briefing mode is currently heuristic while chat remains LLM-backed.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend API base URL is correctly sourced from process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (no hardcoded localhost/internal URLs).
ComparisonPage useEffect dependency warning (stored competitor values) should be addressed to avoid stale comparison fetches if storage values change without remount.
seed_data_creation: No manual seed data created; runtime data generated through tested APIs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated staged homepage UX and key navigation on public URL. Backend endpoints /health, /intelligence/briefing, /latest, /comparison, /chat passed via pytest. Source status currently returns crustdata-live for tested pair and AI=heuristic.
rca of the issue: Low-priority chart warning root cause: ResponsiveContainer briefly computes negative dimensions during initial mount/layout transition, producing repetitive console warnings. Mitigation: ensure stable parent dimensions before chart mount or defer chart render until container size is available.
